content:
I am an Associate Professor of Operations Management at HKU Business School. My research develops stochastic models and data-driven methods to improve decisions in service systems, with a focus on healthcare operations: emergency-department flow, ICU bed allocation, physician scheduling, and patient delay announcements.
Before joining HKU, I was a postdoctoral researcher at Northwestern University’s Department of Industrial Engineering and Management Sciences. I received my PhD in Statistics and Operations Research from UNC Chapel Hill.
summary: I am an Associate Professor of Operations Management at HKU Business School. My research develops stochastic models and data-driven methods to improve decisions in service systems, with a focus on healthcare operations: emergency-department flow, ICU bed allocation, physician scheduling, and patient delay announcements.
title: Huiyin Ouyang

content:
Click on the Slides button above to view the built-in slides feature.   Slides can be added in a few ways:
 Create slides using Wowchemy’s Slides feature and link using slides parameter in the front matter of the talk file Upload an existing slide deck to static/ and link using url_slides parameter in the front matter of the talk file Embed your slides (e.g. Google Slides) or presentation video on this page using shortcodes.  Further event details, including page elements such as image galleries, can be added to the body of this page.
summary: An example talk using Wowchemy's Markdown slides feature.
title: Example Talk

summary: How announced wait time information affects ED overcrowding: a simulation study.
title: Impact of Announced Wait Time Information on Emergency Department Overcrowding Mitigation: A Simulation Study

summary: Structural reform for overcrowding in Hong Kong emergency departments.
title: Beyond Crisis Management: Structural Reform for the Overcrowding in Hong Kong's Emergency Departments

content: What this paper is about Many emergency departments publish expected waiting times to help patients choose where to seek care. Using over 1.3 million patient visits to Hong Kong public EDs, this study estimates how many patients actually respond to these announcements and how sensitive they are to the reported times. The findings inform the design of announcement systems that can reduce waiting times and the number of patients who leave without being seen.
summary: How ED delay announcements affect patient choice and flow in Hong Kong public hospitals.
title: Patient Sensitivity to Emergency Department Waiting Time Announcements

content: What this paper is about When an intensive care unit (ICU) runs out of beds during a surge, hospitals must decide whether to admit a new patient to the ICU or transfer an existing patient to a general ward. This paper develops a Markov-chain model of patient health evolution and proposes admission/transfer policies that minimize the long-run mortality rate.
summary: ICU bed-allocation policies to minimize mortality when demand exceeds capacity.
title: Allocation of Intensive Care Unit Beds in Periods of High Demand
